summary: Exceptional 1968 audience tape with crisp, clear sound captures a high‑energy four‑song set highlighted by a smoking Dark Star, raucous Sittin’ On Top Of The World and a thunderous Death Don’t Have No Mercy.
review: The recording is an audience source that sounds remarkably clean for its era—crisp guitars, clear vocals and just enough room ambience to convey the live vibe. Musically the show delivers a tight, high‑energy performance with Dark Star, Sittin’ On Top Of The World, Good Morning Schoolgirl and Death Don’t Have No Mercy all turning in strong, memorable renditions. While a few reviewers note minor flaws or compare it unfavorably to other early‑68 dates, the consensus is that both the music and the surprisingly good tape quality make this a must‑listen fragment.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviews (5‑star) praise both the performance and the unusually high sound quality for an audience tape; only a few note minor issues, leading to a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with minimal crowd noise; surprising fidelity for a 1968 audience cassette. Some reviewers mention slight echo differences or minor flaws, but overall the tape is described as phenomenal and surprisingly clean.

standout_songs: Sittin' On Top Of The World
show_quality.setlist_flow: A concise four‑song set that captures the high‑energy, exploratory spirit of early ’68, featuring resurrected older numbers and an early truncated Dark Star that flows smoothly into a blues‑rooted Death Don't.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s deep‑throated vocals and expressive guitar work receive repeated praise; his vocal chops are highlighted on Death Don’t.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s spoken quip after Sittin’ On Top is noted (some think the voice sounds more like Phil than Jerry).
band_member_comments.Keys: Simple, single‑keyboard accompaniment is mentioned as part of the raw ’68 sound; no specific praise/critique beyond that.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart’s presence in 1968 is highlighted by one reviewer; another mentions the band sounding “underwater,” but overall drumming is not singled out as a problem.
